title: If a package has the same as the class inside of it then verilator gets confused at using that class in another file (UVM) using import pkg::*

features.name: class with same name as enclosing package used via import
features.description: Code should include a package that contains a class with the same name as the package itself, where that class is referenced via import in another file
features.snippet:
package const_coeff_item; class const_coeff_item; endclass endpackage
package other_pkg; import const_coeff_item::*; class child extends const_coeff_item; endclass endpackage

features.name: class extending parameterized base via import_all
features.description: Code should include a class extending a parameterized base class where the parent class is in a same-named package imported via wildcard
